stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results released early May 2026: FY26 consolidated PAT jumped 91.57% YoY to Rs 1,555.7 cr; revenue from operations up 35.86% YoY to Rs 16,402.7 cr. Q4 EPS beat estimates by +49.22% (revenue miss -0.33%). EBIT margin expanded 370 bps to 14.4%. Reported order executable of $1.75 bn. Management guided robust FY27 growth with consolidated EBITDA margin >20.5%. Trading near Rs 1,284 on May 7 (down 2.27% on May 6 amid IT sector weakness).
stocks.sector_context: Nifty IT index near Rs 29,353 on May 4 (~+0.37% intraday) but down approximately 25% YTD. Primary overhang is GenAI disruption risk from Anthropic, OpenAI, Palantir platforms automating tasks historically delivered by Indian IT services. Despite operational stability and deal wins, valuations have compressed across large- and mid-cap IT.
stocks.peer_comparison: Coforge's 29.2% YoY revenue growth and 370 bps EBIT margin expansion significantly outpace large-cap IT peers (INFY ~3.9% revenue growth, HCLTECH mid-single digit). Premium growth profile but trades at PE TTM ~29.79 versus Infosys ~15.5 and HCLTECH ~19.6.
red_flags: Stock dropped 2.27% on May 6 indicating near-term weakness
Higher PE (~30) vs large-cap IT peers leaves less valuation cushion if GenAI-driven IT derating intensifies
catalysts: FY27 EBITDA margin guidance >20.5% provides ongoing rerating trigger
$1.75 bn executable order book underpins near-term revenue visibility

stocks.recent_news: Trading at Rs 1,196.90 on May 11, 2026. Stock fell 1.25% on May 7 to Rs 1,184.80; broader IT weakness and low volumes (79% of avg) cited. Board recommended Interim Dividend of Rs 24 per share (1200%) at April 21 meeting. Market cap ~Rs 3.25 trillion. Next earnings July 13, 2026.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty IT down approximately 25% YTD on GenAI disruption fears. Sector under continuous pressure as AI platform vendors (Anthropic, OpenAI, Palantir) demonstrate automation of services tasks. TCS holds 36.55% Nifty IT weight; index breadth remains weak.
stocks.peer_comparison: PE ~19.56 is mid-pack vs INFY (~15.5) and Coforge (~30). Recent 5-7 day price action weaker than INFY (which gained 1.3% on May 11). Underperforming on relative basis in last week.
red_flags: Stock declined 1.25% on May 7 with below-average volumes
Sector-wide GenAI overhang continues to weigh on multiples
catalysts: Interim dividend of Rs 24/share (record/payment dates pending)

stocks.recent_news: Stock gained 1.3% to Rs 1,175.70 on May 11, 2026; one of the stronger IT names today. Q4 FY26 reported in late April: revenue up 3.9% YoY, gross profit +7.4%, operating income +6.2%. Completed acquisition of Optimum Healthcare IT on May 5, 2026. Management on US/Canada/Asia investor roadshows May 11-20 (Miami, Toronto, NY, Montreal, SF, HK, Singapore).
stocks.sector_context: Nifty IT down ~25% YTD on GenAI risk narrative. Infosys remains an index heavyweight; sector flows are pressured but quality names with stable margins are being defended.
stocks.peer_comparison: PE ~15.51 is cheapest among large-cap IT names (HCLTECH ~19.6, Coforge ~30). 31.8% ROE among strongest in the sector. Dividend yield 3.8% vs peers offers downside cushion. Today's +1.3% move outperforms HCLTECH (-1.25% on May 7) and broader Nifty IT.
red_flags: Sector-wide GenAI derating still active despite stock-specific strength
Revenue growth (3.9%) trails high-growth mid-cap peers like Coforge
catalysts: Investor roadshows May 11-20 across US, Canada, HK, Singapore - potential positive sentiment driver
Optimum Healthcare IT acquisition closure adds healthcare vertical scale

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 PAT reported at Rs 4,027 cr; FY26 consolidated PAT Rs 14,008 cr; dividend of Rs 0.65 announced. May 2, 2026 earnings call transcript posted on IR website. Stock has been underperforming with 1-year return of -13.2% as of April 30, 2026. PE ~28.12 - elevated for a bank with this growth profile.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty Bank trading near 54,519 on May 11, down 1.43% from prior close. Sector under pressure on subdued global cues and geopolitical concerns. Key technical resistance at 56,300-56,400 zone; support at 55,200-55,400. Banking sentiment cautious in early May.
stocks.peer_comparison: 1-year return -13.2% materially underperforms private bank peers (HDFCBANK, ICICIBANK historically positive 1Y). PE ~28x is among the higher in private bank space, leaving little room for re-rating without earnings acceleration.
red_flags: Nifty Bank weak on May 11 (-1.43%) - sector headwind compounds stock-specific issues
PE ~28x elevated relative to RoE/growth trajectory
catalysts: FY26 results now behind market - next Q1 FY27 results in late July 2026
Any RBI rate decision/liquidity stance could shift sector sentiment

stocks.recent_news: Stock hit a record high of Rs 4,444.9 on May 8, 2026. Q4 FY26 earnings call held May 8. Full year FY26: consolidated revenue +3.13% YoY to Rs 10,694 cr; PBT +13.96% to Rs 1,007.94 cr; PAT +13.56% to Rs 720.50 cr. Q4 EBITDA margin expanded 100 bps YoY to 10.9% (lower inventory costs). Board recommended final dividend Rs 14/share (700%) on May 7. Earnings beat analyst forecasts; consensus EPS upgrades reported.
stocks.sector_context: BSE Capital Goods index at fresh all-time highs (~79,887), up ~17% over two months and ~20% in April alone. Sector tailwind from Union Budget 2026-27 record $133.1 bn infrastructure allocation (roads, ports, railways). Broad-based strength across BHEL, ABB, Polycab, Thermax.
stocks.peer_comparison: Thermax PE ~54-59x is at significant premium to L&T (~25-34x); reflects earnings beat and order book optimism. BHEL has ~Rs 2.4 trillion order book providing peer-group revenue visibility. Thermax up 5.08% intraday on results day; outperforming several large peers.
red_flags: Valuation PE 54-59x is rich vs sector and historical averages - vulnerable to any growth miss
FY26 revenue growth only 3.13% YoY - margin-led earnings, not volume-led
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 14/share pending shareholder approval (record/payment dates upcoming)
Capital goods sector tailwind from FY27 infrastructure capex cycle
Earnings beat triggering consensus EPS upgrades - rerating momentum
